data.title: Ørsted cancels U.K. offshore wind project, warns of hit to earnings
data.description:
Ørsted (OTCPK:DNNGY) said Wednesday it will cancel the Hornsea 4 offshore wind project in the U.K. due to increasing supply chain costs, rising interest rates and construction risks, warning the closure would hurt earnings this year.

The company said ending the project would result in costs of 3.5B-4.5B Danish kroner ($533M-$686M), which would in turn hit EBITDA by 3B-3.5B kroner, including writedowns of offshore transmission assets and contract cancellation fees.

However, Ørsted (OTCPK:DNNGY) reaffirmed guidance for full-year EBITDA guidance of 25B-28B kroner and planned gross capital spending of 50B-54B kroner, unaffected by the Hornsea 4 decision.

Ørsted (OTCPK:DNNGY) also reported Q1 EBITDA rose 14% Y/Y to 8.6B kroner, above expectations of 7.88B kroner in an analyst poll compiled by the company.

Q1 net profit jumped to 4.44B kroner from 2.39B kroner in the year-earlier quarter, and revenues rose to 20.7B kroner from 19.17B, missing consensus of 21.73B kroner.

data.title: CPI Card reports mixed Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• CPI Card Group press release (NASDAQ:PMTS): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.40 misses by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $122.8M (+9.6% Y/Y) beats by $1.9M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA decreased 8% to $21.2 million, compared to the prior year period.
  • As of March 31, 2025, cash and cash equivalents was $31.5 million.
  • CPI affirmed its 2025 outlook of mid-to-high single-digit organic growth for both net sales (vs. estimated growth of 7.54% Y/Y) and Adjusted EBITDA. The Company expects to gain share in its core markets in 2025 and plans to continue to invest in its market expansion strategy.

data.title: IBM Think conference shows 'doubling down' on AI vision: Wedbush
data.description:
The main theme from IBM's (NYSE:IBM) Think conference is that the Arvind Krishna-led firm is “doubling down” on its vision for artificial intelligence, Wedbush Securities said.

“We leave the event incrementally more positive on the IBM growth story heading into 2025 & beyond that the company’s AI and Hybrid Cloud strategy continues to believe that IBM continued to be well-positioned to capitalize on this transformational industry-wide shift,” Wedbush analysts wrote in a note to clients.

They reiterated their Outperform rating and $300 price target on IBM.

At the event, IBM unveiled several new product updates and releases, including the new Z17 Mainframe, Watsonx Orchestrate and more, but it's the benefit that AI is having that may be most impactful, Wedbush analysts posited.

IBM said it has seen a reduction of around $3.5B in costs on an annualized basis over the past two+ years due to increased AI-related productivity, something that has also boosted margins by 5 percentage points. (IBM also disclosed that it is in discussions with Meta (META) for watsonx.AI to become an API provider within its Llama large language model stack.)

The company also has more than 70 workflows infused with AI, including sales, finance and marketing, while still seeing more synergies for its Red Hat and HashiCorp products and integrations.

Additionally, it has saved more than 250,000 work hours due to its AI investments, while its generative AI book has built a backlog of more than $5B.

“IBM remains one of our top software names we would own as our recent checks give us incremental confidence in owning software cloud/AI exposed names, and we are incrementally more bullish on the name moving forward,” Wedbush analysts said.

data.title: Instacart launches a new app for group ordering of party drinks and snacks
data.description:
Instacart (NASDAQ:CART) announced on Wednesday the launch of a new drink and snack delivery app called Fizz aimed at party planning. The app features flat rate delivery, group ordering, and automatic payment splitting.

"Fizz is tailor-made for groups. It takes just one tap to invite others into your Fizz cart, so everyone can choose what they want to bring. Everything is delivered right when you need it, no matter how many people participate, with no membership required," highlighted the company.

Instacart said delivery options with Fizz include beer, seltzer, non-alcoholic drinks, chips, dip, healthy snacks, or party supplies. Based on a customer's delivery address, deliveries can be fulfilled from retailers like Kroger (KR), Albertsons (ACI), Safeway, Schnucks, and Wegmans. The company also highlighted that both hosts and guests can earn rewards on Fizz toward their next order. 

The new initiative from Instacart (NASDAQ:CART) puts in closer competition with DoorDash (DASH) and Uber Eats (UBER), which have reported strong results for alcohol-related delivery offerings.

Looking ahead, Instacart (CART) indicated that it may consider adding more categories to Fizz based on user demands. 

Shares of Instacart (CART) edged 0.6% higher in premarket trading and are up more than 15% since the Q1 earnings report was released.

data.title: Canadian Utilities Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.85

data.title: MasterCraft Boat Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 beats by $0.12, revenue of $76M beats by $1.15M
data.description:
• MasterCraft Boat press release (NASDAQ:MCFT): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 beats by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $76M (-9.5% Y/Y) beats by $1.15M.
  • The decrease in net sales was primarily due to planned lower unit volumes to align dealer inventories with retail demand and changes in price, partially offset by favorable model mix and option sales.
  • Planned decrease in production contributed to approximately 30% lower dealer inventory levels compared to the prior-year
  • For full year fiscal 2025, we now expect consolidated net sales to be approximately $275 million vs $285.65M consensus, with Adjusted EBITDA of approximately $20 million, and Adjusted Earnings per share of approximately $0.71. We also now expect capital expenditures to be approximately $9 million for the year.

data.title: HNI Corporation beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q2 and reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• HNI Corporation press release (NYSE:HNI): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.44 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $599.8M (+2.0% Y/Y) beats by $19.33M.
  • Q1 GAAP operating margin 4.1%; non-GAAP operating margin 5.3% reaches highest Q1 level since 2007
  • Elevated earnings growth visibility remains through 2026 from initiatives already underway
  • Volume growth anticipated for full year 2025 in both segments; 2025 earnings outlook unchanged.

  • Outlook

    • Second quarter net sales. The Corporation expects second quarter 2025 net sales in Workplace Furnishings to increase at a mid-single digit rate year-over-year, including the impact of tariff-related pricing actions. In Residential Building Products, second quarter 2025 net sales are expected to increase at a low-single digit rate compared to the same period in 2024, including the impact of tariff-related pricing actions.
    • Second quarter non-GAAP earnings per share. Non-GAAP earnings per share in the second quarter of 2025 are expected to increase solidly from 2024 levels. This projected improvement is driven by productivity benefits, and volume growth, partially offset by investments and temporary price-cost pressure from tariffs.
    • 2025 net sales. In Workplace Furnishings, the Corporation expects year-over-year mid single-digit net sales growth, excluding the benefit of an extra week in the fourth quarter. In Residential Building Products, the Corporation expects year-over-year low-to-mid single-digit net sales growth, after excluding the benefit of the extra week in the fourth quarter. These projections include the impact of pricing actions implemented to offset tariff pressures. The Corporation’s projection for 2025 net sales and non-GAAP earnings per share are based on current order patterns and leading external indicators, with an acknowledgement that they are subject to elevated levels of uncertainty given current macro conditions.
    • Double-digit EPS growth in 2025 and elevated earnings growth visibility through 2026 vs. estimated growth of 11.11% Y/Y, including tariff impacts. The Corporation's outlook for 2025 earnings is unchanged with double-digit percent EPS growth expected, driven by KII synergies, the ramp-up of the Mexico facility, and increased profits from volume growth. The remaining $0.70 to $0.80 of incremental EPS benefit from KII and Mexico are expected to be roughly evenly distributed between 2025 and 2026, providing strong earnings visibility over the next two years. For the full year, the Corporation expects to offset the majority of any tariff-driven pressures.
    • Balance sheet. The Corporation’s low leverage and strong cash flow generation will provide substantial capacity for capital deployment. Current priorities for capital deployment are reinvesting in the business, funding dividends, pursuing share buybacks, and exploring M&A opportunities.
